plan_summary.plan_name: EU Cross-Border Rail Through-Ticketing Coordination Program
plan_summary.primary_goal: Achieve 40% of eligible cross-border journeys sold as single through-tickets within five years, supported by binding OSDM standards and a solvent clearing mechanism.
plan_summary.modelling_frame: Budget allocation against the EUR 1.5B coordination fund, clearing-mechanism liquidity buffers against working-capital shocks, and through-ticket adoption against the 40% target.

key_values.label: Public coordination budget total
key_values.comment: Total EU coordination fund covering grants, reference distributor, clearing capitalisation, governance, and reserves.
key_values.source_text: Public coordination budget total: EUR 1.5 billion

key_values.label: Capacity-building grants allocation
key_values.comment: 60% of the EUR 1.5B fund earmarked for IT capacity-building grants to national operators.
key_values.source_text: Capacity building grants: EUR 900M (60% of 1.5B) for national operators.

key_values.label: Public reference distributor 5-year cost
key_values.comment: 10% ring-fenced for establishing and running the public reference distributor over five years.
key_values.source_text: Public reference distributor running costs: 10% (EUR 150M) of 1.5B fund.

key_values.label: Initial clearing mechanism float requirement
key_values.comment: Liquidity buffer needed to settle inter-carrier obligations at T+3 across busy corridors.
key_values.source_text: Initial clearing mechanism float: EUR 200 million.

key_values.label: Emergency Float Reserve
key_values.comment: Ring-fenced contingency for clearing-mechanism shocks; activated if liquidity dips below 150% of peak daily obligation.
key_values.source_text: Emergency Float Reserve ring-fenced budget: EUR 300 million.

key_values.label: Through-ticket adoption target
key_values.comment: Primary five-year success metric: share of eligible cross-border journeys sold as single through-tickets.
key_values.source_text: Cross-border journeys sold as single through-tickets target proportion: 40% within five years.

key_values.label: Governance/standards/testing FTEs (first two years)
key_values.comment: 35 FTEs across standards, conformance testing, and stakeholder relations; consumes operational overhead not covered by grants.
key_values.source_text: Personnel capacity: 35 FTEs for governance/standards/testing in the first two years.

key_values.label: Plausible clearing-mechanism liquidity shock
key_values.comment: Mid-point of the stated 50-200M working-capital gap that the public budget may need to absorb under under-capitalisation.
key_values.source_text: Under-capitalisation could force EU budget to cover float gaps of EUR 50 to 200 million.

derived_questions.question: Does the projected through-ticket volume clear the 40% adoption target?
derived_questions.why_it_matters: If projected adoption is below the target, the program's primary success metric is missed.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Total eligible cross-border journeys per year
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Denominator for converting the 40% adoption target into an absolute ticket volume and revenue base.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Triangulate from Eurostat cross-border rail passenger statistics for the four launch corridors, with 30-50% uplift for full EU coverage.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Projected through-ticket adoption fraction at year five
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Realised quantity tested against the 40% adoption target; without it the gate is dead-ended.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Analogy to PSD2/open-banking adoption curves and distributor uptake projections under cost-recovery royalty caps.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Peak daily clearing obligation
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Drives the 150%-of-peak liquidity threshold and so determines the effective coverage of the EUR 200M float.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Estimate from projected daily through-ticket volume times average cross-border fare, with seasonal peak multiplier.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Average monthly cost per governance/testing FTE
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Required to size governance operating overhead against the residual coordination budget.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: EU institution salary benchmarks for senior technical/regulatory staff plus overhead, typically 12-18k EUR per FTE per month fully loaded.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Other program overheads (testing, governance ops, contingencies outside grants/distributor/reserves)
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Captures unallocated EUR 1.5B coordination-fund consumption beyond grants, reference distributor, reserves, and FTE cost.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Assume 5-15% of the EUR 1.5B fund for testing, audits, legal, and contingency outside the named line items.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Governance two-year personnel cost
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Bottom-up FTE cost is the only way to connect the 35-FTE staffing plan to the budget envelope.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Coordination budget buffer after named commitments
recommended_first_calculations.formula_hint: coordination_budget_buffer_eur = public_coordination_budget_eur - capacity_building_grants_eur - public_reference_distributor_cost_eur - emergency_float_reserve_eur - governance_two_year_personnel_cost_eur - residual_program_overhead_eur
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Confirms whether the EUR 1.5B envelope actually covers grants, reference distributor, emergency reserve, governance overhead, and other testing/legal overheads. The EUR 200M operational float is financed via transactional-fee collateral and is not debited from the fund.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Clearing mechanism liquidity surplus under shock
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Tests whether the EUR 200M float and EUR 300M reserve together absorb the stated 50-200M working-capital shock.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Clearing liquidity coverage versus peak daily obligation
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Operationalises the 150%-of-peak-daily-obligation tripwire that triggers reserve activation; ratio >= 1 means the reserve clears the trigger.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Absolute through-ticket volume target
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Converts the 40% adoption target into the absolute ticket count distributors and operators must process at year five.

unmodelled_gates.label: DG MOVE legal authority for OSDM/enforcement
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: Without a binding legal mandate from DG MOVE the 'Three Strikes' enforcement and mandatory API exposure cannot be applied to sovereign operators.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: Standards remain voluntary; major operators ignore deadlines; the 40% adoption target collapses regardless of budget.

unmodelled_gates.label: Incumbent operator political acceptance of enforcement
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: Coordinated lobbying or political resistance by SNCF/DB/OeBB can stall enforcement and the tiered-liability framework outside any modelled threshold.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: 6-12 month delays on key routes and weakened liability framework, undermining clearing-mechanism and adoption assumptions.

unmodelled_gates.label: PRM accessibility schema bound into core standard
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: Failure to bind PRM accessibility schemas into the month-18 standard creates a regulatory equity breach the deterministic model cannot quantify.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: Mandatory non-compliance, fines, and reputational damage that block the program independently of budget or adoption outcomes.
